RiskFactors: No mitigation language inside the same risk narrative; use [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] / [[AI:XREF|to=Regulation]] for mitigation elsewhere.
Use real prospectus risk prose: one risk heading followed by cause/adverse-effect paragraphs. Do not output memo labels such as Factual Cause, Harm to Issuer, or Harm to Investors.
A risk factor is acceptable only if it has issuer-specific context from evidence or is explicitly marked DATA_MISSING with a source request.
Do not produce a risk table or risk matrix; the final output must read like long-form prospectus disclosure.
Business: Mission, vision, strengths, strategies, awards, market leadership, customer scale, R&D scale and product lists may be drafted only when directly supported by retrieved context.
For product/platform catalogues with more than 10 items, group by business segment or platform instead of enumerating every item.
The opening overview must identify business, geography, operating model and scale; if any are missing, use DATA_MISSING instead of generic company prose.

When evidence contains customer/supplier concentration, segment revenue, R&D/IP or data privacy facts, include the corresponding prospectus subsection instead of leaving them buried in generic overview text.
Do not use pitch-deck wording such as scalable, disruptive, world-class, best-in-class or leading unless the exact claim is backed by cited source evidence.
FinancialInfo: Historical analysis only. Do not create profit, margin, cash-flow, valuation or working-capital forecasts.
Use financial tables where multi-period figures exist; if management explanations are absent, write DATA_MISSING rather than inventing period-on-period drivers.
Every period-to-period explanation must state the exact line item, compared periods, amount/percentage movement and evidence pointer.
If income statement data exists but cash flow or balance sheet data is missing, draft the available table and mark the missing liquidity/balance-sheet subsections explicitly.
Do not create non-IFRS measures unless definition and reconciliation evidence are retrieved.
IndustryOverview: Every market size, CAGR, forecast, ranking, market share or consultant statement must include an AI:CITE tag with source, scope, date and metric.
If consultant/source evidence is missing, produce a source-request skeleton; do not draft a generic market essay from common knowledge.
Keep the issuer out of the market narrative except where defining the segment in which it operates or where a sourced ranking/share claim is provided.
Regulation: Do not state legal compliance conclusions unless the retrieved context contains legal-opinion or regulatory evidence. Otherwise use COUNSEL_INPUT_REQUIRED.
ForwardLooking: No specific profit, margin, or dated profitability outlook; generic safe-harbour only unless a formal profit-forecast process applies.
Contents: Must support page numbers and appendices entries; if pagination unknown, mark DATA_MISSING per line — do not fabricate page numbers.
ExpectedTimetable: Must include severe weather / extreme conditions arrangements if applicable; cross-reference Summary-only where required by practice; link to How to Apply and Structure of the Global Offering when timing depends on offering mechanics.
Definitions: Alphabetical ordering of defined terms; terms must match usage across the document.
Glossary: Request for Elaboration (RfE) style for technical terms only; no duplicate legal definitions from Definitions.
UseOfProceeds: Allocation percentages must sum to 100% where percentages are used; otherwise flag COUNSEL_INPUT_REQUIRED.
Each allocation row must include purpose, amount or percentage, implementation/timing and source pointer when available.
Do not invent net proceeds, offer-price assumptions, expansion plans, R&D budgets or acquisitions; missing items must be DATA_MISSING / COUNSEL_INPUT_REQUIRED.
GlobalOfferingStructure: Must interlock with How to Apply and Underwriting (cross-references, consistent mechanics naming).
Waivers: WVR warning text must follow locked counsel blocks when is_wr in issuer metadata.
